violations.text: Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler, and dry storage area in kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Single service items (pizza boxes) observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area in the kitchen. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2025

The walk in floor has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris around a board that is used as a ramp. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (vegetable chopper, food containers on clean storage rack) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Pasta, cut tomatoes, coleslaw, and cheese observed held at 47-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Pasta at 60 degrees F was disposed of. All other foods were placed in refrigerator or freezer to rapidly chill. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
One prep unit was not in good repair, not holding food at 41 degrees or below. Top of prep unit is not to be used to store TCS foods until it has been serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

The walk in floor has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris around a board that is used in place of a ramp at the door. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2025

The ceiling and doors in the warewashing area has an accumulation of food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
The walk in freezer is in poor repair, with excessive ice build-up on the fan soffit. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
Employee observed drinking beverage directly over food prep unit. Observed employee beverages on prep units and food prep counters during food preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen, bar, and toilet rooms were lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, mixer, knives, vegetable chopper, tomato slicer, food containers and utensils on clean storage racks) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All items are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Eggs, salmon, sliced tomatoes, prosciutto, lettuce and batter observed held at 54-70 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Chemicals observed stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Beef was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
ROP fish observed thawing with packaging intact. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Food observed stored on the floor in the downstairs food prep area in front of the hand sink. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses.Knives observed in cracks between prep units and table, prep unit and table, and prep table and hot hold unit. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
Utensils observed stored in water at 92 degrees F.Water at 135 degrees F may be used to store utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in areas of the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Aluminum wrap is used to line part of stove. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:knife rackutensil bins on clean storage rackcan opener basemixerfood scalebar glass storage shelvesbar glass storage freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025
The dumpster enclosure has an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, debris, and wood pallets.The dumpster enclosure must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025
The oil recycling bin has an accumulation of grease and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025
The fan covers in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025

The walls in the dishwashing area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in areas of the kitchen. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025

Dispenser bottle was observed in the hand-washing sink at the cookline. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed to be washed and sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024
The hand-washing sink in the basement prep area was lacking paper towels. PIC provided paper towels during the inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024
The hand washing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024

Raw animal foods (raw beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (shellfish, pre-cooked foods) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024

Chicken was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024

Working containers of various spices were not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC labeled containers during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024

A shaker cup was being used as scoops in ice bin at the bar. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the bar dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024
The bar areas (downstairs) are lacking 3-bay sinks to adequately wash, rinse, and sanitize dishes. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024
The floors in the basement common areas near banquet area were observed with pooled water. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2024
